rationale: Round-2 introduces no regression in the downstream-contract lens I approved. R9 still pins the same `config show --gate <name> --json` top-level members, typed JSON encoding, resolver agreement, additive-only evolution, and recorded `panel.json` `gate` contract. Map-order determinism is unchanged: gate rendering still follows `PanelGateKeys`, and substitution ordering remains structurally controlled rather than map-iteration dependent. The proof change from `grep -c '"slot"'` to `jq '.slots | length'` is a contract improvement, not a behavior change: it verifies the JSON shape consumers 110/111 will decode and no longer depends on pretty-printing or substring layout. The added nil guard and cmd-side advisory test are orthogonal to the R9 schema surface.
